cells.value: At a glance - budget base & AP/LLTM bridge
authoritative top-line; overlaps BC -> non-additive (detail below)
supplier LLTM already inside Basic Construction -> $0 (no double-count)
SCN Annual - P-5c per-FY cost-category breakdown ($M)
GFE Sum (from CSV; preserved per audit chain)
GFE Sum (from CSV; preserved per audit chain)
LLTM AP - P-10 advance-procurement buckets + AP->TAM bridge ($M)
Virginia (LI 2013) - P-10 advance procurement buckets ($M, FY20-FY31)
Columbia (LI 1045) - P-10 advance procurement buckets ($M, FY20-FY31)
Phase-3 reconciliation: P-10 bucket -> TAM treatment (AP/LLTM base = 0, no double-count)
Bechtel BPMI naval reactor - a GFE line, not BC; also out of the BC POP corpus (Phase-3 fix)
WPN/OPN-adjacent ordnance, not SCN ship construction
CMC / missile tubes - GFE / industrial-base, not addressable hull work
engineering / lead-yard design - the Plan Costs line, not Basic Construction
shipyard-procured material booked as direct material INSIDE Basic Construction
economic-order-quantity material - also inside Basic Construction
supplier-addressable HM&E / propulsor material inside Basic Construction
Conclusion: the supplier-addressable LLTM (shipbuilder-procured + EOQ + HM&E + propulsor) is ALREADY
inside the P-5c Basic Construction base, so wiring the P-10 AP as a separate stream would double-count the
BC TAM. The AP/LLTM base is therefore 0 (confirmed, not a placeholder). BPMI naval-nuclear is GFE, so it is
also excluded from the BC coefficient corpus for base<->corpus consistency (BC coeff 75.7% -> 35.0%; see Validation).
P-10 -> TAM reconciliation bridge ($M, FY22-27): gross AP nets to $0 additive
authoritative Advance Procurement total, Va+Col ($44.71B; ties Inputs + Sensitivity)
shipbuilder-procured + CFE + EOQ + HM&E + propulsor - booked as direct material in BC (~-$16.8B)
top-line exceeds the named-bucket detail in early Virginia years (~-$0.6B); still P-10 AP that overlaps BC -> non-additive
no outside-supplier AP that isn't GFE, already in BC, or un-itemized overlap -> confirmed $0
Note: the ~$18.8B AP/LLTM POP corpus (POP_Corpus) is a different lens - the gated-POP denominator for the 48.5% reference coeff - not the P-10 gross above; both reconcile to $0 additive.
